whyKalkan.title: Why Kalkan?
whyKalkan.description: A whitewashed harbour town stacked above a turquoise bay — rooftop restaurants, hidden coves, and some of the clearest water on the Mediterranean. Quiet enough to unwind, lively enough to never run out of dinner plans.

facts.label: to Kaputaş, one of Turkey's most photographed beaches

guideTeaser.description: Beaches, boat trips, ancient ruins, and where to eat — our local guide covers the best of the coast.

aboutPage.title: A small collection, cared for personally
paragraphs: Villa Kalkan started as a small collection of villas along the Kalkan coastline, brought together in one place so travelers could compare them side by side before booking. We don't run a booking platform of our own — every stay is reserved directly through Airbnb or Booking.com, so you keep the protections and support you'd expect from either.

What we add is local knowledge: which villas suit families, which are quiet enough for a work trip, and which have the best view for sunset. If you're not sure which villa fits your trip, reach out and we'll help you pick.

values.title: Hand-picked, not mass-listed
values.description: Every villa in our collection is chosen personally for its location, privacy, and view — we don't add a villa just to grow the list.

values.title: Book where you already trust
values.description: We list on Airbnb and Booking.com so you get their protections and reviews — this site just makes it easier to compare our villas first.

values.title: Local support, real people
values.description: Questions before or during your stay go to someone who knows Kalkan, not a call center.

faqPage.title: Frequently asked questions
faqPage.description: The short version: browse here, book on Airbnb or Booking.com, and ask us anything in between.

items.q: How do I actually book a villa?
items.a: You book on Airbnb or Booking.com. Each villa page here links straight to its live listing — you complete the reservation there, with the platform's payment protection, cancellation policy, and support. We never take payment on this site.

Yes — each villa's calendar on this site syncs directly with its Airbnb and Booking.com calendars. If a date shows as available here, it's bookable on the platform. If you spot a mismatch, the platform calendar is always the source of truth.

items.q: Why book through Airbnb instead of directly with you?
items.a: Trust. Platform bookings give you buyer protection, verified reviews, secure payments, and a dispute process. We'd rather you feel completely safe than save a commission.

items.q: Do I pay more by going through this website?
items.a: No. This site is just a showroom — the price you see on the Airbnb or Booking.com listing is exactly what you'd pay finding it there yourself.

items.q: What time is check-in and check-out?
items.a: Typically check-in from 4pm and check-out by 10am, but each listing states its exact times. Early check-in or late check-out can often be arranged in shoulder season — message us or ask via the platform.

items.q: Are the villas suitable for children?
items.a: Most are — several have gardens, shallow pool ends, and cots available. Each villa page lists its amenities, and we're happy to advise which villa best fits your family.
All villas are professionally cleaned before arrival. Mid-stay cleaning varies by villa — some include it, others offer it on request. Check the listing or ask us.
It helps. Villas in Kalamar and İslamlar are a 5–10 minute drive from town; central villas are walkable. Taxis and airport transfers are easy to arrange, and we can recommend local hire companies.

items.q: Which airport should I fly into?
items.a: Dalaman (DLM) is closest — about 1h45 by road. Antalya (AYT) is an alternative at around 3 hours. We can put you in touch with trusted transfer drivers who meet you at arrivals.

items.q: When is the best time to visit Kalkan?
items.a: May to October. July and August are hottest and busiest; May–June and September–October offer warm seas with quieter streets and easier restaurant bookings.

faqPage.replyNote: We usually reply within a few hours —

guidePage.title: A week in Kalkan, done right
guidePage.description: Everything our guests ask us — the beaches worth the drive, the tables worth booking, and the days out worth planning ahead.
guidePage.heroAlt: Sunset over the Mediterranean from the hills above Kalkan

swim.title: The beaches
swim.description: Three very different ways to spend a day in the water, all within half an hour of your villa.

explore.title: Days out
explore.description: When you're ready to leave the pool — which, fair warning, takes a few days.

eatDrink.title: Where to eat
eatDrink.description: Kalkan punches far above its size for food — these are the three experiences not to miss.

beaches.name: Kaputaş Beach
beaches.detail: The famous turquoise cove wedged between cliffs on the Kalkan–Kaş road. Arrive before 10am in high season; the water is unreal.

beaches.name: Patara Beach
beaches.detail: 18 km of protected sand dunes beside Lycian ruins — one of the longest beaches in the Mediterranean, and a sea-turtle nesting site.

beaches.name: Kalamar Bay
beaches.detail: Beach clubs on wooden platforms over deep, clear water. Sunbeds, ladders straight into the sea, and lunch a few steps away.

experiences.name: Private gulet day
experiences.detail: Charter a traditional wooden boat from the harbour and spend the day swimming in coves you can't reach by road.

experiences.name: Saklıkent Gorge
experiences.detail: Wade through an 18 km canyon of cold mountain water — a perfect escape on the hottest days.

experiences.name: Xanthos & Letoon
experiences.detail: UNESCO-listed Lycian ruins twenty minutes away, usually with hardly another visitor in sight.

experiences.name: Kaş day trip
experiences.detail: Kalkan's bohemian neighbour — pretty streets, small galleries, and diving schools, 30 minutes along the coast road.

dining.name: Rooftop dinners
dining.detail: Kalkan is famous for its rooftop restaurants — book a table above the old town for sunset over the bay.

dining.name: Harbour front
dining.detail: Fresh fish, meze, and people-watching right on the marina. Busy in season; reserve ahead.

dining.name: Village breakfasts
dining.detail: Drive up to İslamlar village for a traditional Turkish breakfast with trout farms and valley views.

seasons.detail: Warm seas, green hills, quieter streets. Ideal for couples.
Peak summer: 30°C+, buzzing harbour, book villas early.
Still swimming weather, softer light, easier tables.

options.name: From Antalya Airport (AYT)
options.detail: About 2.5 hours by road to Kalkan.

options.name: From Dalaman Airport (DLM)
options.detail: About 2 hours by road to Kalkan — the closer of the two.

options.name: Shuttle transfer
options.detail: Regional shuttle services run to Kalkan for an extra fee.

options.name: Dolmuş (shared minibus)
options.detail: Local dolmuş minibuses also run directly to Kalkan.
